name: Cortex Internal Developer Portal API
what_it_does: Internal Developer Portal (IDP) platform with REST API — enabling platform engineering teams to catalog services, APIs, and resources, enforce engineering standards via scorecards, provide self-service infrastructure provisioning, and give developers a unified view of their software ecosystem, with integrations into GitHub, PagerDuty, Datadog, and other DevOps tools via API.
use_cases: Cataloging microservices and APIs via Cortex API for service discovery and ownership mapping
Enforcing engineering standards via Cortex scorecards API for software quality compliance
Tracking service health and SLOs via Cortex API across the engineering fleet
Providing self-service provisioning via Cortex scaffold API for new service creation
Integrating DevOps tool data via Cortex API for unified engineering intelligence
not_for: Open-source IDP requirements (use Backstage for open-source, self-hosted alternative)
Small teams without platform engineering investment (Cortex is enterprise-focused)
Pure monitoring/alerting (use Datadog or PagerDuty for observability)
best_when: A growing engineering organization (50+ engineers) wants a managed IDP with scorecards and engineering standards enforcement without operating Backstage infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need an open-source self-hosted IDP (Backstage), have a small team, or want complete infrastructure control.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Cortex account settings. No OAuth2. Webhooks for scorecard changes and catalog events. Entity (service/resource) creation and updates via REST API. YAML entity descriptor files also supported (CortexYAML). GraphQL available via partner integrations.

pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2019. $45M+ funding (Series B 2022). Backed by Sequoia and Tiger Global. Positioned as managed alternative to Backstage. Key differentiator: scorecards — rate services on engineering standards (test coverage, on-call, security scans). Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, PagerDuty, Datadog, Snyk, SonarQube, LaunchDarkly, and 50+ more. CortexYAML: YAML files in repo for service definition. Scaffold: templated service creation. GitOps-native approach. Used by Spotify, NBCUniversal, and other engineering organizations. Competing with OpsLevel, Port, and Backstage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Webhooks. Scorecards are key feature. Enterprise pricing. No free tier. Sequoia-backed. 50+ integrations. CortexYAML for GitOps. Annual contract.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Entity reads idempotent by entity tag. Scorecard reads idempotent. Entity creation/update idempotent by entity tag (PUT creates or updates). API operations are generally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Entity tag (not ID) is the primary identifier — use tag for all entity references
CortexYAML in repo is alternative to API for entity definition — team may use both
Scorecards evaluate entities across rules — can only fail if entity is missing required data
50+ integrations must be configured before API data from those systems is available
Enterprise pricing — budget validation required before procurement process
US data residency — verify for EU compliance requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth. SOC2 certified. Service catalog data (ownership, dependencies, on-call) is operationally sensitive. US data residency. Webhook payload validation. Sequoia-backed enterprise pedigree. No OAuth2 scope granularity.
